title: Do not print directly to stdout in Commit()
body: Buildah is being used as a library in Podman, and as such should avoid printing directly to standard streams. Instead, return an image ID which can be printed by the caller (if desired)

comments.body: Actually you changed the functionality.  If I specify --iidfile then it should not output the ID>

I have a strong feeling this change breaks the CI, although I could be wrong.
There are 9 failed tests on both Red Hat CI and travis-ci:
* from-by-digest-s1
* from-by-digest-s1-a-discarded-layer
* from-by-tag-s1
* from-by-repo-only-s1
* from-by-digest-s2
* from-by-tag-s2
* from-by-repo-only-s2
* commit-to-from-elsewhere
* push

@mheon would you mind to check it out. :smile:

This looks like https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/commit.go#L125 throwing an error. Before this patch, this was allowed to fail, and the ID just wouldn't print in that case.
@nalind is it expected that we sometimes can't retrieve the committed image like that? If so, returning the ID would have best-effort, and that error can be made nonfatal.
@rhatdan fwiw, I think this one should also be in v1.0 if at all possible.
It's expected that we can't read the image's ID when the destination isn't in local storage, since we're reading the ID from the record kept by local storage for an image with the target name.
Alright, I'll change it to be a best-effort return
LGTM assuming happy tests.  Thx for working this through @mheon.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 44468f8) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
